title: [phase:critique] [family:audit-hardening] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Critique the prototype's audit design. Return top 5 hardening steps to move from append-only JSONL + fsync toward stronger tamper evidence without overbuilding v1. No edits.
summary_text: Current audit design is solid for durability, but weak for tamper evidence: it appends + `fsync`, yet has no cryptographic linkage, no immutable anchor, and some fail-open audit paths ([tmux_wrapper.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-wrapper-prototype/tmux_wrapper.py#L151), [tmux_wrapper.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-wrapper-prototype/tmux_wrapper.py#L344), [openclaw-tmux-replacement-evidence.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-replacement-evidence.md#L267)). Top 5 v1 hardening steps (no overbuild): 1. Add per-record hash chain + sequence number. - Add `seq`, `prev_hash`, `entry_hash` over canonicalized record fields. - Detect edit/delete/reorder with minimal code change. 2. Add periodic signed checkpoints (every N records or 1 min). - Sign latest `entry_hash` with Ed25519 (key in Keychain/secure store). - Store checkpoint locally + one independent sink (e.g., repo artifact/object store) for anti-rewrite evidence. 3. Harden the audit file open path. - Use `os.open(..., O_APPEND|O_CREAT|O_WRONLY|O_NOFOLLOW)` and verify owner/perms/inode. - Force `0600` file and `0700` dir; reject symlink/hardlink/path traversal. 4. Tighten fail-closed behavior for audit outages. - Keep read-only actions optionally fail-open, but make all mutating and deny decisions fail-closed when audit write/chain state is unhealthy. - Add `--strict-audit` default-on for automation. 5. Ship an `audit verify` command + tamper tests. - Verify full chain/tail, checkpoint signatures, monotonic `seq`. - Add tests for truncation, line edit, line deletion, reordering, and checkpoint mismatch; gate release on these. Workflow note: this critique is single-lane Codex output; per your Kernel policy, treat that as fallback violation and run parallel non-Codex review lanes before finalizing the hardening plan.
